sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR, created_at DATE, value INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, value) VALUES (1, 'Test1', DATE '2023-01-01', 10);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, value) VALUES (2, 'Test2', DATE '2023-01-02', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, value) VALUES (3, '', DATE '2023-01-03', 3);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, value) VALUES (4, 'Test4', DATE '2023-01-04', -5);
SELECT right(name, 3) || ' ' || (DATE '2023-01-01' + INTERVAL value DAY) AS result FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT name, right(COALESCE(name, ''), value) AS right_value FROM test_table WHERE created_at > DATE '2022-12-31';
SELECT id, right(name, 2) AS suffix, created_at + INTERVAL (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table) DAY AS new_date FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT right(name, 2) AS short_name FROM test_table WHERE LENGTH(name) >= 3 ORDER BY short_name;
SELECT COUNT(*), right(name, 1) FROM test_table GROUP BY right(name, 1) HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, right(name, 5) + INTERVAL value DAY AS extended_date FROM test_table WHERE value < 5 AND name != '';
